vulnerability.reference: Human feedback raters share demographic and cultural biases that skew reward signals.
vulnerability.generated: Users assume reward-aligned outputs generalize to unseen prompts, lacking monitoring for distributional drift.

impact.reference: Factual inaccuracies in generated images erode user trust in AI for professional communication tasks.
impact.generated: Incongruent images erode user trust and block adoption of efficient one-step models at scale.

dose_response.reference: Online RLHF post-training enables distributional shift, which in open-domain generation exposes users to degraded image quality on unseen prompts, leading to factual inaccuracies and digital cultural erasure.
dose_response.generated: One-step text-to-image models optimized via reward-based noise or prompt editing produce misaligned outputs, through users in open-domain generation tasks, triggers assumption of generalization without monitoring distributional drift, leading to incongruent images eroding trust and blocking adoption.
